sourceTitle: How to create a content calendar for your marketing team

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using a single Notion content calendar for a marketing team?

options.text: It automatically writes all copy for you
options.explanation: Notion provides a workspace, not AI‑generated copy.

options.text: It centralizes all content types, assets, and status updates in one place
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes that a single calendar lets blogs, videos, social posts, and more live together with status, assets, and feedback.

options.text: It replaces the need for any other marketing tools
options.explanation: Notion complements other tools; it doesn’t replace every specialized platform.

options.text: It guarantees higher SEO rankings
options.explanation: While SEO checklists can be added, the calendar itself doesn’t guarantee rankings.

questions.question: When creating the initial database for the calendar, which Notion view does the guide recommend starting with?

options.text: Table view
options.explanation: Table view is an option, but the guide focuses on a board view for visual status columns.

options.text: Board view (Board‑Full page)
options.explanation: The guide instructs to type /board and select Board‑Full page as the first step.

options.text: List view
options.explanation: List view isn’t mentioned as the starting point for the calendar.

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: Calendar view is useful later, but the initial setup uses a board.

questions.question: Which property type would you use to assign a specific team member to a piece of content?

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select is for predefined categories, not people.

options.text: Date
options.explanation: Date stores deadlines, not people.

options.text: Person
options.explanation: The guide suggests using a Person property called “Writer” to tag the responsible teammate.

options.text: Checkbox
options.explanation: Checkbox is for binary flags, not assigning users.

questions.question: How can you make certain properties appear directly on the cards in a board view?

options.text: Edit the page’s header hierarchy
options.explanation: Headers affect page formatting, not card visibility.

options.text: Click the ••• menu on the board, choose Properties, and toggle the desired ones
options.explanation: The guide explains that toggling properties in the board’s ••• menu shows them on cards.

options.text: Create a linked database and set it to display those fields
options.explanation: Linked databases are for separate views, not for showing properties on board cards.

options.text: Add a formula property that concatenates the values
options.explanation: Formulas generate new values but don’t control card display.

questions.question: What advantage does grouping columns by a property (e.g., content type) provide?

options.text: It automatically publishes the content to social media
options.explanation: Grouping is a visual organization tool; it does not publish content.

options.text: It lets you see all items of the same type in the same column, making status tracking easier
options.explanation: Grouping by a property rearranges columns so each column represents a value (e.g., Blog, Video) for easier overview.

options.text: It locks the board so no one can move cards
options.explanation: Grouping does not affect edit permissions.

options.text: It converts the board into a calendar view
options.explanation: Grouping changes column organization, not view type.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a ‘linked database’ as used by the Notion marketing team?

options.text: A copy of the original database that updates independently
options.explanation: A linked database reflects the original and updates in sync, not independently.

options.text: A view of an existing database placed on another page, allowing customized filters and sorts
options.explanation: The guide says they keep a view of the content calendar on the team wiki—a linked database.

options.text: A database that automatically syncs with external tools like Google Sheets
options.explanation: Linking refers to internal Notion views, not external sync.

options.text: A special type of page that cannot be edited
options.explanation: Linked databases are fully editable unless permissions restrict them.

questions.question: When building a template for a new piece of content, where do you locate the option to create it?

options.text: At the top of the board, click the blue “New” button’s down arrow and select “+New template”
options.explanation: The guide specifies this exact location for creating a template.

options.text: Right‑click any card and choose “Create Template”
options.explanation: Right‑click does not open a template creation menu.

options.text: In the Properties menu, click “Add Template”
options.explanation: Templates are created from the New button, not from Properties.

options.text: From the Share menu, select “Make Template”
options.explanation: Sharing settings are unrelated to template creation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a hard‑level concept discussed in the guide?

options.text: Using a Person property to assign writers
options.explanation: This is a basic property usage, not a hard concept.

options.text: Relating a content database to a social‑media database via a relational property
options.explanation: The guide mentions linking two databases with a relational column to see associated social posts, which requires understanding of relational properties.

options.text: Changing the board view to a list view
options.explanation: Switching view types is straightforward.

options.text: Adding a checkbox for an SEO checklist
options.explanation: Checkboxes are simple property types.

questions.question: If you want the public to find your Notion page via Google, which setting must you enable?

options.text: Toggle “Share to the web”
options.explanation: Sharing makes the page public, but it does not guarantee indexing.

options.text: Enable “Search engine indexing” after sharing to the web
options.explanation: The guide says you can toggle Search engine indexing to make the page discoverable on Google.

options.text: Add a public tag to the page title
options.explanation: There is no tag that controls indexing.

options.text: Create a linked database on a public site
options.explanation: Linked databases affect internal views, not external search visibility.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best captures why the guide suggests customizing multiple views for the content calendar?

options.text: To hide certain content types from the entire team
options.explanation: Views are for filtering, not permanently hiding content from everyone.

options.text: To provide tailored slices of the calendar (e.g., weekly launches, social media only) that different team members can quickly access
options.explanation: The guide explains that custom views let you filter and sort for specific workflows, like weekly product launches or social media calendars.

options.text: To duplicate the database into separate databases for each project
options.explanation: Views stay within the same database; they don’t create new databases.

options.text: To automatically assign tasks to team members based on view
options.explanation: Views are for display; task assignment requires properties or automation.
